整套首页
unit_07 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_07

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 320 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Text Completion
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
communalelaboratemaintainvicious
communal: standard unit headword | elaborate: standard unit headword | maintain: standard unit headword | vicious: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
Defying expectation, the electorate seems poised to (i) __________ a number of political tenets so (ii) __________ that they are often paraded as fact.Defying expectation, the electorate seems poised to (i) __________ a number of political tenets so (ii) __________ that they are often paraded as fact.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　reject; entrenched
defyintricateentrench
defy: standard unit headword | intricate: standard unit headword | entrench: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)______ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.During the Harlem Renaissance, Alain Locke (i)______ the first flourishing of a self-consciously racial art movement in America and was widely credited with providing the philosophical basis for its emergence. His importance as a critic of African America art and as an art theorist is (ii)______ if controversial, yet he has received (iii)______ attention for his unique insight into the broad forces that shaped American modernism and cultural nationalism in the visual arts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　presided over; undisputed; insufficient
meticulousmisleadingshape
meticulous: standard unit headword | misleading: standard unit headword | shape: standard unit headword
#4 Sentence Equivalence
In denying him a promotion, management cited his ______ decision making, charging that his judgements were based on vagaries rather than careful forethought.In denying him a promotion, management cited his ______ decision making, charging that his judgements were based on vagaries rather than careful forethought.
答案：A, D　capricious; cavalier
managementdogmatic
management: standard unit headword | dogmatic: standard unit headword

Although the governor was not optimistic about placating the opposing factions, he nevertheless hoped that his statement would have __________ effect.Although the governor was not optimistic about placating the opposing factions, he nevertheless hoped that his statement would have __________ effect.
答案：A　an irenic
polemicalplacate
polemical: standard unit headword | placate: standard unit headword
#6 SE
The Harvard Observatory took the first picture of a star in 1850, but the complex logistics of photography at the time-involving coating glass plates with chemicals-made it too to photograph stars nightly.The Harvard Observatory took the first picture of a star in 1850, but the complex logistics of photography at the time-involving coating glass plates with chemicals-made it too to photograph stars nightly.
答案：B, D　cumbersome; unwieldy
unwieldycumbersome
unwieldy: standard unit headword | cumbersome: standard unit headword
#7 SE
Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.Unlike schools of European modernism, the United States Black Arts Movement of the 1960s did not __________ its purpose or aesthetic and thus produced a heterodox array of art ranging from abstraction to social realism.
答案：B, D　prescribe; codify
camouflagerealism
camouflage: standard unit headword | realism: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture

This cautious approach makes diplomatic sense, since attempts over the past 15 years to reach international agreements on patents have (ii)______ just such sensitive issues.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　contentious; foundered on

答案：E　vehement

Despite the (i)______ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)______ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)______ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.Despite the (i)______ in negotiations apparently signaled by the recent agreement between the two neighboring countries, the countries remain (ii)______ even about the import of that agreement. One wants to prolong agreed-to resumption of limited cross-border traffic, believing it can become entrenched as a new status quo. The other, by contrast, insists that the resumption is (iii)______ and has been undertaken voluntarily and provisionally in order to show goodwill.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　progress; unreconciled; temporary
entrenchstalematerequisite

Blank 2

sanguinemaintainexacerbate

#14 Text Completion
A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.A hallmark of certain nineteenth-century mystery novels was the reform agenda of their authors, who ostensibly sought to expose economic injustice while depicting the seamy underside of urban life. In reality, however, these claims to a radical political agenda were often (i)______, meant to give lurid thrillers the appearance of (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　disingenuous; high-minded purpose
suspenseagenda
suspense: standard unit headword | agenda: standard unit headword
#15 TC TRIPLE
The recent finding is particularly (i) ___________, given that climatologists predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.The recent finding is particularly (i) ___________, given that climatologists predictions about sea-level changes are based on the expectation that if the ice sheet lost mass at its edges, its overall mass would increase, since rising temperatures would lead to more snowfall over the continent's midsection. However, if overall mass is actually (ii) __________, it will mean that climatologists predictions for the rise in the sea level for the coming century are (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　ominous; decreasing; seriously understated
particularly
particularly: standard unit headword

Although its director ______ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.Although its director ______ that the movie uses a documentary approach in portraying the famous sit-down strike, in practice its characters are heavily fictionalized and fall into familiar Hollywood types.
答案：A, F　asserts; maintains
maintainassert

While this terminology de-emphasizes the (ii) ___________ aspects of knowledge management processes, such elements abound in the actual data collection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　neutrality; interpretative and evaluative
collectmanagement

#18 TC SINGLE
The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.The Internet is an eternally unfinished monument to __________, a text that aspires to be as big and multifarious as the world it describes.
答案：A　inclusion
multifarious
multifarious: standard unit headword
#19 TC TRIPLE
Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.Since readily available computer programs now allow photographers to manipulate their images with the same (i) __________ as painters, photography's claim to any inherent truth has just about evaporated. But the (ii) __________ possibilities of photography have been present from the medium's birth. Distortions of truth not only occur mechanically (through shutter speed, for example) and in printing (as through choice of chemicals and paper) but are (iii) __________ to the very idea of photographers being stylistically different from one another.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　license for fiction; fictive; integral
license
license: standard unit headword
#20 Sentence Equivalence

答案：D, F　anticipates; prefigures
overture
overture: standard unit headword

答案：A, D　espousing; championing
lambaste

答案：B　Arbitrariness
reciprocity

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed
facetious
facetious: standard unit headword
#25 Sentence Equivalence
As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, ______.As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, ______.
答案：A, B　superficial; sketchy
at best
at best: standard unit headword

Their (ii) the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.

Blank 2
答案：A, E　extolled; collective infatuation with.

verbose

答案：D, E　stubborn; intractable

Blank 3

The committee called for restrictions not just on chemicals that are hazardous according to the standard definition (which equates hazard with toxicity) but on all chemicals that are persistent and that accumulate in the biosphere. Toxicity is an (i)________ criterion, the committee argued, since evidence of toxicity (ii)________ public health.

Blank 2

equate

答案：D, E　elisions; lacunae

sweeping

Blank 2
答案：C, F　sentiments; Inadequate for

答案：C, F　salubrious; optimal
salubrious

答案：A　crude

equanimity

Is there a contemporary filmmaker who is genuinely devoted to great literature? In this age of puerile Hollywood productions, it may be (i) a few serious intellectuals from the roster of glib, lowbrow directors. But to find a director (ii) great books-and able to make them into great movies-takes some searching.

Blank 2

答案：C, E　tortuous; convoluted
tangible

答案：B, C　a cosmopolitan; an inclusive

Blank 2

答案：B, D　uncertain; tenuous

答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from
untether

Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)______ claims regarding the (ii)______ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)______. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell

#51 Text Completion
The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative
purveyor
purveyor: standard unit headword

As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her ______; rather than representing fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
humility
humility: standard unit headword
#53 Text Completion
While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite ______, Lytton Strachey's irreverent 1918 essay about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.While early biographies of Florence Nightingale tended to be quite ______, Lytton Strachey's irreverent 1918 essay about her ushered in a new era, making it acceptable, even fashionable, to criticize her.
答案：B　sycophantic
usher
usher: standard unit headword
#54 Text Completion
The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a ______ exercise in fiscal prudence.The governor might conceivably find a genuine resolution to the budgetary dilemma, but she may be tempted to engage in a deception: a ______ exercise in fiscal prudence.
答案：C　specious
blatant
blatant: standard unit headword
#55 Sentence Equivalence
Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.Excessive focus on what might have been can cause in us feelings of restlessness and regret, but some scientists are beginning to think that fancying an alternative reality might have ______ effects as well.
答案：C, F　restorative; tonic
tonic
tonic: standard unit headword

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.Scientific papers often (i)______ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account

#57 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in

Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.It is possible for human to go 40 or more hours without sleep and still be able to (i)______ information acquired at the beginning of the sleepless period. Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.

答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce

#67 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles
manage
manage: standard unit headword

The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) ____________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) ____________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated
diffuse
diffuse: standard unit headword

Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii)________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii)________.The label "melodrama” tends to have (i)________ implications when used to describe a film with historical subject matter. Melodrama is widely perceived as antithetical to cinematic realism and thus, from a perspective that seeks (ii)________ rendering of the historical past, incapable of delivering the necessary (iii)________.

Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　pejorative; a seemingly unmediated; objectivity

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) ____________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) ____________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) ____________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound

It is an effective (ii)_______ the (iii)_______ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey’s book is representative of a heightened sense of (i)_______. #102 SE
The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.
答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate
intricate
intricate: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
Adam Smith's defense of capitalism was (i) __________ but (ii) __________: Smith strongly endorsed it over all previous socioeconomic systems as the one in which the most people possible could live decent lives, yet he was not the dogmatic advocate of free enterprise that he is so often thought to have been.Adam Smith's defense of capitalism was (i) __________ but (ii) __________: Smith strongly endorsed it over all previous socioeconomic systems as the one in which the most people possible could live decent lives, yet he was not the dogmatic advocate of free enterprise that he is so often thought to have been.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　resolute; qualified
dogmatic
dogmatic: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.The environmentalist asserted that although the trend in building regulations was theoretically geared toward energy efficiency, in terms of energy consumption, new buildings typically were becoming more __________ every year.
答案：C　profligate
assert
assert: standard unit headword

Moreover, Earth Day--a global holiday meant to celebrate the wonder of life on the planet--has (ii) ___________ the frivolous "picnic holidays," its significance (iii) __________ the smoke of barbecue pits and backyard grills.In some countries, filibustering and stall tactics have held improvements to air and water standards at bay, leaving environmental activists (i) ___________ . Moreover, Earth Day--a global holiday meant to celebrate the wonder of life on the planet--has (ii) ___________ the frivolous "picnic holidays," its significance (iii) __________ the smoke of barbecue pits and backyard grills.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　dispirited; joined the ranks of; obscured by
placate
placate: standard unit headword

The laboratory maze has grown ever less ______ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.The laboratory maze has grown ever less ______ since it was invented; instead of hoping to lose a rodent in a labyrinth, today’s scientists design mazes to elicit a few simple, easily measurable behaviors.
答案：A, B　intricate; convoluted

intricate: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　instability; F fluidity

It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.At its best, the storytelling in this television series manages to accommodate a sense of historical (i) __________. The narrative shows that American independence was not (ii) __________. It was debated. It didn't happen overnight. No one was sure how it would turn out.

答案：A, D　contingency; inevitable

The computer modelers who are their potential collaborators can (iii)_______ by taking a dismissive attitude toward scientists who have relatively little quantitative training.

. For Native Americans, song traditionally has had a _________ element: definite results--such as the bringing of rain, success in battle, or the curing of the sick--are expected from music.. 答案：D, F　paucity; dearth
particularly
particularly: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　drive; suspense

Blank 2

No matter how much input orchestra players are allowed in choosing a music director, they never feel they have enough. Regardless, their criticisms of Webster's expertise and stature within the field are nothing short of (iii) __________. Would they really prefer some of the other potential candidates whose names have been released?It's possible that the (i) __________ of the musicians about the director's appointment is mostly an expression of (ii) __________ the selection process. No matter how much input orchestra players are allowed in choosing a music director, they never feel they have enough. Would they really prefer some of the other potential candidates whose names have been released?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　carping; disgruntlement regarding; preposterous

stimulating: standard unit headword
#115 TC SINGLE
Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.Increased dopamine is positively correlated to impulsivity; if the connection is causal, then higher levels of melatonin from exposure to more darkness at night should __________ impulsive behaviors, since higher levels of melatonin cause dopamine to drop.
答案：D　inhibit
exacerbate
exacerbate: standard unit headword

During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying ______.During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying ______.
答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude
realism
realism: standard unit headword

答案：B, F　a rigorous; a faithful
license

Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented

答案：D　pristine

There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them ______ to most readers.There are many insights in the essays collected in Observations on Modernity, but they are embedded in a dense English translation of a dense German original that may make many of them ______ to most readers.
答案：B, F　inaccessible; opaque

答案：B, F　diffuse; scattered

To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii)_______. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i)_______, mathematicians are (ii)_______ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii)_______. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　reliable; skeptical regarding; appear to work

Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.Conservationists tend to be (i) ____________ framing their case in terms of personal feelings; if pushed to describe the moral roots of their convictions, they generally give (ii) ____________ arguments for species preservation, claiming that diversity is intrinsic to ecosystem health, which is in turn intrinsic to human survival.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　uncomfortable with; utilitarian

答案：C　capricious

答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated

答案：B, F　correcting; redressing

But this agricultural archive is (i)______ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world’s most (ii)______ places.

Blank 2

答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes

Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) rather than (ii) . That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (ii) their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly ______ group of thinkers.Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly ______ group of thinkers.
答案：D　contentious

答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional

答案：A, B　overblown; inflated

#156 TC DOUBLE
Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous
sporadic
sporadic: standard unit headword

Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.Analyses of Tracy K. Smith’s poetry that (i)_________ its formal and aesthetic qualities are (ii)________, as Smith’s formal choices are ultimately (iii)_______ her political arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　prioritize; deficient; ancillary to
intricate
intricate: standard unit headword
#158 Text Completion
In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.
答案：B　obscure
discourse
discourse: standard unit headword
#159 Sentence Equivalence
As a historical genre, biography is best when ______, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.As a historical genre, biography is best when ______, a careful reconstruction of the past in all its unfamiliar particularity.
答案：E, F　meticulous; thorough
meticulous
meticulous: standard unit headword
#160 SE
The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to
replete
replete: standard unit headword
#161 TC TRIPLE
Rosenfeld’s study, while treading over some relatively well-known ground, offers a (i)_______ perspective on its contours. For one, her treatment of Leonard Woolf’s life is serious and respectful, going some distance in (ii)_______ his portrayal as a baleful monster and as a relentlessly (iii)_______ influence on his wife Virginia.Rosenfeld’s study, while treading over some relatively well-known ground, offers a (i)_______ perspective on its contours. For one, her treatment of Leonard Woolf’s life is serious and respectful, going some distance in (ii)_______ his portrayal as a baleful monster and as a relentlessly (iii)_______ influence on his wife Virginia.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　fresh; revising; deleterious
affable
affable: standard unit headword
#162 Text Completion
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
captivate
captivate: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.The idea of traveling in order to find natural extremes and wonders was (i) __________ in eighteenth- and nineteenth-century Europe, and many European travelers of the period were drawn to Iceland and other North Atlantic countries as places where they might find these (ii) __________ landscapes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　not uncommon; exotic

答案：C, E　private; insular
pursuit
pursuit: standard unit headword
#165 SE
The fierce drought that is gripping the area-and imminent rationing and steep water price increases---is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.The fierce drought that is gripping the area-and imminent rationing and steep water price increases---is __________ the area's deep economic divide, as wealthy communities guzzle water while poorer communities nearby conserve by necessity.
答案：C, E　highlighting; underscoring

exacerbate: standard unit headword
#166 Text Completion
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
entrench
entrench: standard unit headword
#167 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
agenda
agenda: standard unit headword

The professor’s habitual air of ______ was a misleading front, concealing amazing reserves of patience and a deep commitment to his students’ learning.The professor’s habitual air of ______ was a misleading front, concealing amazing reserves of patience and a deep commitment to his students’ learning.
答案：B, F　irascibility; exasperation
misleading
misleading: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　dogmatic; orthodox

#170 Text Completion
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

答案：C, E　unimpeachable; blameless

答案：B, F　collective; social

Substantial atmosphere around planetary bodies acts as (i)______ incoming objects. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. Smaller objects, particularly those that are lower in density and more fragile, (ii)______ in the upper reaches of the atmosphere, whereas more intact, larger bodies may survive to impact the surface. 答案：B, E　exalt; valorize

答案：D, E　mordant; acerbic

Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook

#214 Text Completion
Holston characterized a colonial situation as an aggregation of activities and a conjunction of outcomes that, though ______ and at times coordinated, were usually diffuse, disorganized, and even contradictory.Holston characterized a colonial situation as an aggregation of activities and a conjunction of outcomes that, though ______ and at times coordinated, were usually diffuse, disorganized, and even contradictory.
答案：D　linked
diffuse
diffuse: standard unit headword
#215 Sentence Equivalence
There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.
答案：C, D　novel; original
intricate
intricate: standard unit headword
#216 Text Completion
Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.Behavioral economists have come to believe that a (i)______ of choices can be paralyzing as Schwartz pointed out in the recent book The Paradox of Choice. Studies of retirement plans show that the more investment choices a plan offers, the less likely people are to participate in it. It may follow, then, that a lack of flexibility in certain plans may actually be a (ii)______ . People reasonably (iii)______ some advantages in exchange for peace of mind.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　surfeit; virtue; forgo
surfeit
surfeit: standard unit headword
#217 Sentence Equivalence
Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.
答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed
multifarious
multifarious: standard unit headword
#218 Text Completion
Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i)______ but downright (ii)______.Making loans and fighting poverty are normally two of the least glamorous pursuits around, but remarkably enough put the two together, and you have an economic innovation that has become not just (i)______ but downright (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　popular; chic

pursuit: standard unit headword
#219 Sentence Equivalence
When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that ______ logic.When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that ______ logic.
答案：E, F　defied; disregarded
defy
defy: standard unit headword

答案：B　esoteric
pursuit
pursuit: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　obscure; opaque

misleading: standard unit headword

Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)______. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)______ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)______, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)______. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)______ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)______, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：C, F　a nebulous; a vague

答案：C　tenuous

答案：B, D　precarious; perilous

答案：A, E, G　shaped by; condemn; salutary

答案：A, B　abound; proliferate

Blank 1

答案：B, F　limitation; objectivity

答案：C, F　detracting; deprecatory

#232 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility
humility

答案：C, F　obsolete; outmoded

The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)______ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)______ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)______ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)______ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)______ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)______ by the frogs.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：A, E　insatiable; voracious

#242 Text Completion
Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive
manage
manage: standard unit headword

Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded

They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)______. They typically seek to (ii)______ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.

Blank 2

In addition to providing documentation that demolishes the all-male version of prospecting, Zanjani uses the examples of her female loners to (ii)______ some of the (iii)______ generalizations about Euro-American women as uniformly nurturant and sociable pioneers.Women in the mining towns of the American West were strictly stereotyped into neat categories of public and private, good and bad, but the 100 intrepid female prospectors in Zanjani’s book managed to (i)______ those categories. Blank 2

#265 Text Completion
Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved souvenirs; the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere effort
license
license: standard unit headword
#266 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
particularlyexacerbate
particularly: standard unit headword | exacerbate: standard unit headword

Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. The public loved the souvenirs the critics (ii)______ what they saw as his betrayal of artistic integrity. Haring claimed that these critics misunderstood his (iii)______ to take art beyond galleries and museums, and thus to give a wider audience the opportunity to experience art.Keith Haring’s cartoonish art became even more (i)______ than Andy Warhol’s much-reproduced soup cans when, in 1986, he opened his own store, the Pop Shop, to sell licensed Haring merchandise. 答案：A, E, I　commercial; decried; sincere need

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

答案：C, F, H　appealing; useless; highly valuable
intricate
intricate: standard unit headword
#311 Text Completion
Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose
pretentious
pretentious: standard unit headword

Research note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______ : if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.Research note that wolves’ otherwise strongly hierarchical society is marked by occasional displays of populist (i)______ : if a pack leader proves a too-snappish tyrant, subordinate wolves will (ii)______ the top cur.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow

答案：C　prestige

While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i)______ are also (ii)______ : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance.While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i)______ are also (ii)______ : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　omissions; telling
misleading

#315 Text Completion
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______ , communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______ , communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
communalelaboratemaintainvicious
communal: standard unit headword | elaborate: standard unit headword | maintain: standard unit headword | vicious: standard unit headword
#316 Text Completion
In the solar system, collisions involving cosmic objects are among the most ______ processes shaping surfaces: images of many solar system objects show a proliferation of impact craters formed throughout the past 4.5 billion years.In the solar system, collisions involving cosmic objects are among the most ______ processes shaping surfaces: images of many solar system objects show a proliferation of impact craters formed throughout the past 4.5 billion years.
答案：B　pervasive

Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report
maintainshape
maintain: standard unit headword | shape: standard unit headword
#318 Text Completion
The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
suspenserealism
suspense: standard unit headword | realism: standard unit headword

The stories in Yiyunshi’s recent collection are distinctive, particularly for the strong contrast between their emotional intensity and their consistently ______ tone.The stories in Yiyunshi’s recent collection are distinctive, particularly for the strong contrast between their emotional intensity and their consistently ______ tone.
答案：C　measured

#320 Text Completion
Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more ______, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.Some of the areas of research covered in the collection have already attracted substantial scholarly interest, while others are more ______, hence requiring pioneering effort to map the territory and suggest productive avenues of inquiry.
答案：B　pristine
